title: cicuta nodiflora
intro: Cicuta nodiflora, also known as water hemlock or poison parsnip, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species that belongs to the carrot family. Traditionally, its medicinal properties have been utilized in various herbal remedies, although with great caution due to its toxic nature. Despite the risks, Cicuta nodiflora has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. However, its preparation and consumption are highly regulated, and it should only be handled and used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional or herbalist.
